description:
A SINGLE UNINTERRUPTED PANORAMIC PAINTING of one continuous antique dealer's cabinet interior, rendered as ONE flowing seamless mural with absolutely NO vertical lines dividing the image, NO triptych frames, NO panels, NO image seams, NO black borders between any parts — strictly forbidden to split the image into thirds or sections; the result must read as a single oil-painted scroll-like view of the same room.

IMPORTANT compositional constraint: this panorama will be viewed on a smartphone that swipes horizontally, snapping to one of three viewing windows. Each viewing window shows roughly a third of the image (0–33%, 33–66%, 66–100% of the width). For this reason, NO key element (no door, no window, no piece of furniture, no major architectural feature) may straddle the vertical positions at 33% or 66% of the image. The areas around 33% and 66% must contain ONLY plain continuous wallpaper / empty wall surface — they are the 'breathing zones' where one viewing window transitions to the next.

Architecture (described continuously across the width): the viewer looks into a cozy three-walled interior. At the far LEFT (roughly 0–18%), a LEFT SIDE WALL angles forward toward the viewer. From 18% to 82% we see the FLAT BACK WALL straight on. From 82% to the right edge, a RIGHT SIDE WALL angles forward toward the viewer. The walls meet at TWO interior corners (around 18% and 82%) — visible only as a soft change of wall plane and gentle painted shading at the join, never as a hard line. ONE continuous wooden parquet floor runs unbroken across the bottom. ONE continuous ceiling moulding runs unbroken across the top. Vintage damask wallpaper covers the entire continuous wall surface.

Feature details by horizontal position (0% = left edge, 100% = right edge):

• 0–22% — STRONG FOREGROUND ANCHOR: a substantial MAHOGANY WRITING DESK juts forward from the LEFT-BOTTOM CORNER of the image into the viewer's space, the front edge cropping out of frame on the left. We see the near front corner of the desk and its full top surface from a slight top-down 3/4 angle, with the desk oriented so that its long side runs from the left-foreground back-right toward the back wall — a person sitting at this desk would FACE the back-wall window (see below). On the desk surface: a few scattered sheets of parchment, a brass inkwell with a quill pen, a small green-shade Art Déco desk lamp lit warmly, a leather-cornered blotter pad. Leave generous bare areas on the desk top so that small additional objects (a rolled newspaper, a notebook) can be composited on later.
• 0–18% — LEFT SIDE WALL behind the desk: a TALL BUILT-IN BOOKSHELF running floor-to-ceiling, packed with old leather-bound books and a brass bookshelf rail. The bookshelf is on the angled side wall, partly behind the desk.
• 22–30% — on the BACK WALL: a tall WOODEN-FRAMED WINDOW set at normal ground-floor height (NOT high under the ceiling — sill around mid-wall, around the desk surface height), with multiple panes divided by wooden mullions. The interior of the panes shows a PAINTED VIEW of a Parisian street at GROUND-FLOOR level, NOT a view of rooftops or sky alone: we see across the cobbled street to the opposite side, where a classic Haussmannian building facade with stone balustrades and tall french windows runs along the street; chestnut tree trunks and lower foliage along the sidewalk; a wrought-iron gas streetlamp; perhaps the suggestion of a passer-by silhouette. Soft impressionistic brushstrokes, warm pastel midday daylight. Same Art Déco rendering as the rest of the image (sepia ink + soft wash). This window is the one the desk faces.
• 33% area — only plain continuous damask wallpaper (BREATHING ZONE for the swipe boundary between the desk view and the door view). No door frame, no window, no furniture here.
• 42–58% — on the BACK WALL: a heavy wooden FRONT DOOR with a polished brass letterbox slot at mid-height, set flush into the back wall, no protruding frame breaking the wall plane. EXTREMELY IMPORTANT: the door's vertical centerline MUST be at EXACTLY the 50% horizontal position of the entire panorama — perfectly centered to the left/right edges of the image. The door occupies horizontal 42–58% (8% padding on each side from its centerline). A thin Persian runner on the parquet floor in front of the door leads up to it, also centered. The composition around the door must be symmetric.
• ~50% on the CEILING: an ART DÉCO BRASS PENDANT CEILING LAMP (plafonnier) hangs down from the center of the ceiling above the door — a multi-tiered chandelier with frosted glass tulip shades casting a warm pool of light down toward the door. The lamp's chain stays well above the door, never crossing into the floor area.
• 66% area — only plain continuous damask wallpaper (BREATHING ZONE for the swipe boundary between the door view and the fireplace view). No window, no fireplace edge, no furniture here.
• 70–78% — on the BACK WALL: a second tall WOODEN-FRAMED WINDOW set at the SAME ground-floor height as the left window (sill around mid-wall), symmetrical to the one at 22–30%. The interior of the panes shows the PAINTED VIEW continuing further down the same Parisian street at GROUND-FLOOR level: more of the opposite Haussmannian building facade with a corner café terrace and round bistro tables on the sidewalk, more chestnut trunks, a second wrought-iron gas streetlamp. Same warm pastel midday daylight, same impressionistic Art Déco rendering style. NO rooftops, NO sky-only view — keep the street level visible.
• 82–100% — RIGHT SIDE WALL with a STONE FIREPLACE (small warm fire burning in the hearth, carved stone mantelpiece with a few decorative trinkets, chimney column rising to the ceiling). The fireplace front faces the viewer at 3/4 angle. A large Persian rug on the parquet floor in the foreground (78–100%), a small wooden side table, and a clear empty floor space (no objects) reserved for an armchair that will be composited later.

Lighting: soft warm directional light from the upper-left, consistent across the whole panorama (back-wall windows + desk lamp + pendant lamp + fireplace all reinforce a cozy lit interior). Cohesive Art Déco rendering: cream parchment paper texture, sepia ink line-art, soft forest green wash, brass and warm wood tones, subtle paper grain. No people, no text, no captions, no signatures, no watermark.

CRITICAL FINAL CONSTRAINTS — (a) Both back-wall windows show a painted view of the SAME Parisian street at GROUND-FLOOR level (across the street: Haussmannian facades, chestnut trees, sidewalk, gas lamps). Absolutely NO rooftops, NO sky-only view, NO view from above — we are at street level. No transparent areas anywhere in the image — the PNG is fully opaque. (b) Absolutely no vertical seams, no black bars, no panel borders anywhere in the image. (c) NOTHING (no furniture, no door, no window, no architectural feature) crosses the vertical position at 33% or 66% of the image — those zones contain only continuous damask wallpaper. (d) The front door is PERFECTLY CENTERED at the 50% horizontal position of the image, with the rest of the composition symmetric around that centerline.

A heavy wooden front door, closed, with a polished brass letterbox slot at mid-height. The door is intended to be composited on top of the CENTER zone of the reference image (the entrance wall), at the exact same position, perspective and scale as the door already shown in the reference. Match the reference's eye-level perspective, the soft warm light from upper-left, the rendering style (Art Déco line-art + sepia/forest green wash on cream parchment), and the door's apparent height in the reference. Isolated subject only — door panel, frame and letterbox visible — on transparent background, nothing else.

A vintage tufted Chesterfield armchair in deep emerald-green velvet, three-quarter front view facing slightly toward the viewer. The armchair is intended to be placed on the Persian rug in the RIGHT zone (reading nook) of the reference image, in the empty floor space between the side table and the fireplace. Match the reference's eye-level perspective, scale (the armchair should fit naturally on the rug without overlapping the fireplace), and lighting (soft warm light from upper-left). Use the same Art Déco line-art + sepia/forest green wash style on cream parchment. Isolated subject on transparent background — armchair only, no rug, no nook elements.

A rolled-up vintage newspaper tied with a string, lying flat on a desk surface. The newspaper is intended to be placed on the mahogany writing desk in the LEFT zone of the reference image, viewed from the same slight top-down angle the desk surface has. Match the reference's perspective, scale (the rolled newspaper should look like a regular ~40cm log on the desk), lighting from upper-left, and the same Art Déco line-art + sepia/forest green wash style. Isolated subject on transparent background — newspaper only, no desk.

A small leather-bound notebook, closed, brown cover with gilded edges, lying flat next to the rolled newspaper on the same writing desk. The notebook is intended to be placed on the mahogany writing desk in the LEFT zone of the reference image. Match the reference's perspective (same slight top-down angle as the desk), scale (notebook ~A6 size on the desk), lighting from upper-left, and the same Art Déco rendering style. Isolated subject on transparent background — notebook only, no desk.

ONLY the vintage gramophone itself: a polished wooden box (the soundbox/mechanism, ~30cm wide) with a polished brass tulip horn pointing to the upper-left. ABSOLUTELY NO furniture, NO pedestal, NO side table, NO support of any kind under the gramophone — the wooden box appears to rest flat on an invisible surface, ready to be composited on top of the small wooden side table that already exists in the reference image. Three-quarter front view, eye-level perspective matching the reference, warm soft light from upper-left, same Art Déco line-art + sepia/forest green wash style on cream parchment. Isolated subject on transparent background — wooden gramophone box + brass horn ONLY, nothing else.

A single closed white envelope as if dropped on the floor, slightly rotated, soft shadow underneath. Intended to be placed on the floor right in front of the door in the CENTER zone of the reference image. Match the reference's perspective on the floor (slight top-down) and lighting from upper-left. Same Art Déco rendering style. Isolated subject on transparent background — envelope only.
